original_abstract:
This research aims to establish the foundational framework for "quantum law" as a visionary legal paradigm. This necessity arises from the unique physical properties of quantum phenomena and the specific risks and challenges inherent in quantum technologies. By examining current regulatory landscapes both within the Russian Federation and internationally, the study identifies the urgent need for a legal structure capable of governing these emerging advancements.

The authors employed a multidisciplinary methodology, integrating systemic analysis with specific techniques from engineering and legal theory. A comparative-legal approach was utilized to map the divergent trajectories of quantum technology regulation across different jurisdictions. Furthermore, legal modeling was used to conceptualize the structural components of quantum law, including its core ethical and legal principles.

The study identifies the transformative properties of quantum technologies that threaten to outpace existing legal frameworks and highlights the critical risks associated with their proliferation. Key findings include a detailed synthesis of international regulatory trends and the identification of specific developmental vectors within the Russian legal system. Consequently, the research articulates the conceptual pillars of quantum law, outlining the essential pathways for its future evolution.

This article represents a pioneering effort in legal scholarship by providing a comprehensive analysis of national and international quantum regulations to justify the birth of quantum law as a distinct field. Practically, these findings offer a blueprint for future legislative and enforcement efforts, providing policymakers and researchers with the theoretical tools necessary to navigate the complexities of the quantum era.
humanized_abstract:
The purpose of this research is to develop a theory base for the concept of the “quantum law” as a futuristic legal model. There is an essential need for a new regulatory framework dealing with quantum phenomena and the associated risks as well as dangers emerging in the course of the quantum technology implementation. An analysis of the existing legislation for Russia and foreign countries has shown that it is vital to establish a legal framework for the development of quantum technologies.

The authors used a multidisciplinary approach by integrating systems analysis with various techniques and methods from engineering and law. They used a comparative-legal method for mapping the development of quantum technology regulation in different countries and legal models for designing the structure of quantum law, including its underlying ethical and legal norms.

The study explores the consequences of quantum technologies, which can fundamentally change and are developing at a rate that current legislation is not capable of regulating and even pose a number of global risks. The study makes a systematic analysis of current regulatory practices in various countries and outlines the main lines of development of the Russian legislation in this area, which allows to summarize the main concepts of quantum law and to outline the main vectors of its further development.

This article is the first in legal scholarship to consider national and international quantum regulation and thereby make a case for the emergence of a new branch of law — quantum law. In practical terms, the article provides a roadmap for any future quantum legislation and enforcement activities and thus equips lawmakers and other researchers with the legal theories they will need to cope with the many new challenges that the quantum age is bound to bring.
